structural_claim: Information loss and thermodynamic behavior emerge from fundamental dynamics in systems with horizons or boundaries that create causal disconnection

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Black holes obey thermodynamic laws with entropy proportional to horizon area, revealing information loss at causal boundaries
Gravitational collapse erases all information except three parameters
Color confinement creates a boundary beyond which quark information cannot propagate, similar to how horizons trap information
Nucleons lose their individual identity when bound in nuclei, representing information loss in the nuclear medium
